trivia_bullets.text: 'The first mistake the Grimnant knights made was bullying the owner of an Urborg corpse shop. Their second mistake was dying.' Deadpan humor: the knights insulted the wrong person and conveniently provided the material for their own comeuppance. 'Urborg corpse shop' implies the swamp-kingdom had an active trade in undead commodities under Cabal rule.

During Dominaria, Urborg had been partially occupied by the reformed Cabal under Belzenlok; a resistance faction called Free Urborg operated against the Cabal. The corpse shop owner in the flavor text occupies the grey zone: an independent operator in the Cabal's shadow economy.
sources: Wizards of the Coast Return to Dominaria story series

trivia_bullets.text: Soul Salvage was reprinted in Core Set 2020 (M20-116) and Jumpstart (JMP-280). The Dominaria version's flavor text is unique to DOM; M20 has different flavor text. Pauper-legal and penny-dreadful-legal across all versions.

Illustrated by Daarken (Mike Dalton), who maintains a professional portfolio at daarken.com where Soul Salvage is listed among his Magic work. Known for deep blue-black palettes with high-contrast spectral highlights.

related_cards.name: Soul Salvage
related_cards.note: M20-116 reprint with different flavor text; same mechanic
